_why: Memory orchestration policy for Open WebUI long-term memory and in-context working memory. Designed for long-running technical conversations where prior decisions must remain accessible without exceeding the 65K free-tier context budget.

long_term_memory.why: Loading memory silently prevents conversational disruption. Users do not need to know memory was consulted unless a conflict is found.

write_policy.why: Only decisions and preferences worth recalling in a future session should be persisted. Raw conversation content is ephemeral.

conflict_resolution.prompt_template: This differs from [prior decision]. Do you want to update or override it?
conflict_resolution.why: Silent overwrite of prior decisions corrupts long-term context. Surfacing the conflict preserves user agency.

compression.why: At turn 15, raw transcript typically exceeds 8K tokens of working context. Compressing resolved threads to summaries recovers ~60% of that budget for future turns.

working_memory.why: NIM Free Tier degrades severely above 65K tokens. The context_budget_enforcer filter enforces this hard ceiling before each request.

expiration.why: Memory items older than 90 days without reinforcement are likely stale. Confidence decay prevents old decisions from overriding newer ones silently.

token_budget.why: Memory items should never consume more than 5% of total context budget. At 65K ceiling, that is 3276 tokens. 2048 is conservative and leaves headroom.
